Problem Statement: For the following exercises, convert the parametric equations of a curve into rectangular form. No sketch is necessary. State the domain of the rectangular form.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t^{2} - 1 , & y = \frac{t}{2}\end{matrix}\right .$$
o3 Answer:
The parameter \(t\) can be eliminated as follows  

Given  
 x = t² – 1  
 y = t/2 ⇒ t = 2y  

Substitute \(t = 2y\) into the expression for \(x\):

 x = (2y)² – 1  
 x = 4y² – 1.

Hence the rectangular (Cartesian) equation of the curve is  

 x = 4y² – 1

or, solved for \(y\),

 y = ± ½√(x + 1).

Domain  
Because \(t\) (and therefore \(y\)) can be any real number, 4y² ≥ 0. Thus  

 x = 4y² – 1 ≥ –1.

Therefore the domain of the rectangular form is  
 x ∈ [–1, ∞) (if \(x\) is taken as the independent variable).

Domain of the rectangular form

Hence  
Rectangular equation: x²/16 + y²/9 = 1  
Domain (in x): –4 ≤ x ≤ 4.  (Correspondingly, –3 ≤ y ≤ 3.)

 (x − 1)² + (3 − y)² = 1

 (y − 3)² = 1 − (x − 1)²

the square-root term is real only when  
 1 − (x − 1)² ≥ 0  ⇒ (x − 1)² ≤ 1  ⇒ 0 ≤ x ≤ 2.

Hence the domain (allowable x–values) of the rectangular form is  

 0 ≤ x ≤ 2  (and correspondingly 2 ≤ y ≤ 4 for the y–values).
